much of it. It was only revived as a pilgrimage centre 100 years ago.
	Soon after leaving Rocamadour we passed a campsite with swimming pool in a pretty field, & decided to stay there for the night. **/** "Camping le lot"
It had a small swimming pool & a lovely old stone barn. Other features which attracted the boys were the pingpong table, breadcroissant, badminton, playgrounds.
	In the morning we returned to __Rocamadour__ to watch the eagles. They have a remarkable birds sanctuary — everything from black kites which eat grasshoppers, vultures, owls, & eagles. The head man — a power maniac; stood us out in the hot sun & harangued us for ages on the ideals of his zoo — saving birds, pronature antipollution.... Finally the birds started flying. They were remarkable — they come when one of
pages.notes: Text begins mid-sentence, continuing from a previous page. 'Camping le lot' appears to be the campsite name, possibly 'Camping le Lot' (referring to the Lot river/region). Asterisks '**/**' appear in the margin beside the campsite entry. 'breadcroissant' may be two words 'bread + croissant' run together. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
the keepers raises his gloved arm. One fish-eater, skimmed across a pool to scoop up a fish. Others swooped in front of us from tower to tower. One bird was put on John's & Peter's heads. 2 [?cuts] eagles went off into the sky & refused to come back. They were still messing about when we left.
	On the way to __Casanourse__ **** we stopped to book in at Le Beau Sejour at Calvinet. They wanted us at 8 at the latest. People seem to eat earlier down here — there were already people eating when we walked in.
	When we reached the campsite we swam & Eve did a load of wash — the da 4 days of clothes, one load, for £2. Such a relief. The next day I put all the bed sheets & pyjamas in.
	At Calvinet we had our best meal yet. Eve had Charlotte-cold, subtle-tasting mold of cucumber & potato,
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 129 visible at top-right. 'Casanourse' is underlined and followed by asterisks — could read as 'Casaubon', 'Cassagnes', or similar French place name; 'Casanourse' is best reading from the script. 'Le Beau Sejour' is a restaurant or hotel at Calvinet. 'the da 4 days' likely reads 'the da[rn] 4 days' — 'da' may be an abbreviation or partial word. 'Charlotte-cold' appears to be a dish name (Charlotte froide perhaps). Text ends mid-sentence continuing to next page. '2 [?cuts] eagles' — the word before 'eagles' is struck through and unclear.
